title: Catalyst Watch: China drama, big banks report, Oracle and Nvidia events, end-of-life day for Windows 10
content:
[Wall Street Aerial]
Art Wager/E+ via Getty Images

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - OCTOBER 13

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Rigetti Computing (RGTI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RGTI]) and USA Rare Earth (USAR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/USAR]) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Churchill Capital Corp. X (CCCX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CCCX]), GigCapital7 (GIG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GIG]), and Nuburu (BURU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BURU]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Stellus Capital (SCM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SCM]), TaskUs (TASK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TASK]), and Great Elm Capital (GECC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GECC]). 
 	* POLITICS WATCH - Investors will have their heads on a swivel to see if the U.S. and China can get closer to a trade deal. Meanwhile, the stalemate between Republicans and Democrats over the government shutdown will roll into its second full week.
 	* IPO WATCH - HW Electro KK (HWEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HWEP]) is expected to price its IPO and begin to trade during the week. The quiet periods expire on StubHub (STUB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STUB]), Netskope (NTSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTSK]), WaterBridge Infrastructure (WBI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WBI]), Pattern Group (PTRN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PTRN]), and ChowChow Cloud International (CHOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CHOW]) to free up analysts to post ratings. The IPO lockup period expires for certain blocks of shares of Chagee Holdings (CHA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CHA]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include AbbVie (ABBV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABBV]), Abbott Labs (ABT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABT]), Delta Air Lines (DAL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DAL]), and Colgate-Palmolive (CL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CL]).
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Fastenal (FAST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FAST]).
 	* ALL DAY - OPEC will issue its monthly oil market report.
 	* ALL DAY - The Nvidia Corporation Open Compute Project (OCP) Global Summit 2025 will showcase Nvidia's (NVDA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVDA]) latest innovations in accelerated computing, including AI infrastructure, networking, energy efficiency, and security.
 	* ALL DAY - The four-day Cloudflare Connect conference will begin. Cloudflare (NET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NET]) management will discuss AI, cybersecurity, and cloud infrastructure topics.
 	* ALL DAY - Oracle (ORCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ORCL]) will hold its flagship conference, recently retitled Oracle AI World. The event focuses on advancements in cloud, AI, and enterprise technology, featuring high-profile keynote speakers such as Oracle Chief Technology Officer Larry Ellison.
 	* 12:10 P.M. Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Anna Paulson will speak before the National Association for Business Economics 67th Annual Meeting.

TUESDAY - OCTOBER 14

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Albertsons Companies (ACI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ACI]), BlackRock (BLK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BLK]), Citigroup (C [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/C]), Domino's Pizza (DPZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DPZ]), Goldman Sachs (GS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GS]), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JNJ]), Wells Fargo (WFC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WFC]), and JPMorgan Chase (JPM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JPM]).
 	* ALL DAY - Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will deliver a speech in Philadelphia at the National Association for Business Economics Annual Meeting. The topic will be the "Economic Outlook and Monetary Policy."
 	* ALL DAY - General Mills (GIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GIS]) will hold an Investor Day event. Members of the leadership team will discuss the company's strategic priorities and long-term growth plans.
 	* ALL DAY - The European Commission extended phase review of the Boeing (BA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BA])-Spirit AeroSystems Holdings (SPR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPR]) deal will expire.
 	* ALL DAY - The launch window opens for Rocket Lab (RKLB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RKLB]) to launch its Electron rocket for Japan-based satellite data and analytics company, Symspective.
 	* ALL DAY - Microsoft (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]) will end Windows 10 support, which could leave hundreds of millions of PCs unprotected. For a fee, Windows 10 users can enroll in one more year of security support. Dell Technologies (DELL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DELL]), HP Inc. (HPQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPQ]), Lenovo (OTCPK:LNVGY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LNVGY]), and Best Buy (BBY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BBY]) have been identified as potential beneficiaries of the laptop and desktop replacement being kicked into a higher gear.
 	* 9:00 A.M. The three-day Salesforce Dreamforce 2025 event will begin in San Francisco. Salesforce (CRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRM]) CEO Marc Benioff, Alphabet (GOOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOG]) CEO Sundar Pichai, and Starbucks (SBUX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SBUX]) CEO Brian Niccol will all give talks.
 	* 3:30 P.M. Boston Federal Reserve Bank President Susan Collins will speak and participate in a moderated question-and-answer session before the Greater Boston Chamber of Commerce.

WEDNESDAY - OCTOBER 15

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include ASML (ASML [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ASML]), Bank of America (BAC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BAC]), Morgan Stanley (MS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MS]), Abbott Labs (ABT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABT]), United Airlines (UAL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/UAL]), and PNC Financial (PNC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PNC]).
 	* ALL DAY - Brown-Forman Corporation (BF.A [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BF.A]) (BF.B [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BF.B]) will host an investor conference in Nashville, Tennessee. The event will feature a live webcast of presentations by members of the company’s executive leadership team and Jack Daniel’s leaders focused on the Jack Daniel's Family of Brands’ strategic priorities and long-term growth.
 	* ALL DAY - Dollar Tree (DLTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DLTR]) will hold its Investor Day event.
 	* ALL DAY - Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPE]) will hold an analyst meeting to review the company's strategy and long-term financial outlook.
 	* ALL DAY - Salesforce (CRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRM]) will hold an Investor Day as part of its Dreamforce conference.
 	* ALL DAY - The NACS Show Expo will begin. The gathering is considered the premier annual event for the convenience and fuel retailing industry. Companies showing off new products include Mars Wrigley, General Mills (GIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GIS]), Molson Coors Beverage Company (TAP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TAP]), Hershey (HSY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HSY]), and Mondelez International (MDLZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDLZ]).
 	* 8:30 A.M. The Consumer Price Index report for September is scheduled to be released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. Economists expect the core inflation rate to hold steady at +3.1%.
 	* 1:00 P.M. Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller is scheduled to give a speech on AI at the 2025 DC Fintech Week conference.
 	* 2:00 P.M. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.

THURSDAY - OCTOBER 16

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TSM]), Charles Schwab (SCHW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SCHW]), U.S. Bancorp (USB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/USB]), CSX (CSX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSX]), and Travelers (TRV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TRV]). Options trading implies a double-digit share price move for ManpowerGroup (MAN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAN]) after it reports.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The U.S. Census Bureau is scheduled to release the September Retail Sales report.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The Philadelphia Federal Index report will be released.
 	* ALL DAY - Veeva Systems (VEEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VEEV]) will host its investor day virtually.
 	* ALL DAY - Jefferies Financial (JEF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JEF]) will host its annual Investor Day event in Manhattan.

FRIDAY - OCTOBER 17

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include American Express (AXP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AXP]), Truist Financial (TFC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TFC]), and Ally Financial (ALLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ALLY]).
 	* ALL DAY - It is the last trading day before the beginning of the blackout period for Federal Reserve Open Market Committee members ahead of the next meeting of the central bank.
 	* ALL DAY - The three-day TwitchCon event begins. The event holds some relevance for investors, particularly those interested in the digital media, gaming, creator economy, and advertising industries. Amazon (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]) purchased Twitch in August 2014 for $970 million.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The September Housing Starts report is scheduled to be released by the U.S. Census Bureau and the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development.

_Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story._

MORE ON THE MARKETS 

 	* This Isn't 1999: The AI Supercycle [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4829068-this-isnt-1999-the-ai-supercycle]
 	* Don't Waste Your Time Timing Bubbles: Subscriptions Make This Market Nothing Like 1999 [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4829113-dont-waste-your-time-timing-bubbles-subscriptions-make-this-market-nothing-like-1999]
 	* These Statistics Are A Real Problem For The Bears [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4829108-these-statistics-are-a-real-problem-for-the-bears]
 	* Ray Dalio warns of debt surge, brewing ‘civil war’ in U.S. [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4503222-ray-dalio-warns-of-debt-surge-brewing-civil-war-in-u-s]
 	* SA analysts weigh in on the current government shutdown [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4503103-sa-analysts-weigh-in-on-the-current-government-shutdown]

title: Catalyst Watch: Eyes on housing names, Costco earnings, global PMI reports and key M&A votes
content:
[World Trade Center and Oculus in Lower Manhattan, NYC]
AndresGarciaM/iStock Editorial via Getty Images

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - SEPTEMBER 22

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Tesla (TSLA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TSLA]) and Wolfspeed (WOLF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WOLF]) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on an increased level of options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Churchill Capital Corp. X (CCCX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CCCX]), Willow Lane Acquisition (WLAC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WLAC]), and Tourmaline Bio (TRML [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TRML]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include FS KKR Capital (FSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FSK]), FactSet (FDS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDS]), and Oxford Square (OXSQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OXSQ]). 
 	* ALL WEEK - Investors may have their eyes on the housing sector due to the expectation for lower mortgage rates and improved affordability. 
 	* IPO WATCH - HW Electro (HWEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HWEP]) is expected to price its IPO and begin trading during the week. The quiet period expires on Tryhard (THH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/THH]) and Picard Medical (PMI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PMI]) to free up analysts to post ratings. StubHub (STUB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STUB]) heads into week two of trading.
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Firefly Aerospace (FLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FLY]) will issue its first earnings release as a publicly traded company.
 	* FED SPEAKERS - New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will participate virtually in a panel. Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack will speak on "The Fed's Role in the Economy and Impact on Everyday Life." Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin will speak virtually on the economy before the Howard County Chamber of Commerce.
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Broadcom (AVGO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AVGO]), Logitech (LOGI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LOGI]), Humana (HUM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HUM]), and Ralph Lauren (RL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RL]).
 	* ALL DAY - Robinhood Markets (HOOD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HOOD]), AppLovin (APP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/APP]), and Emcor Group (EME [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EME]) officially join the S&P 500 Index.
 	* ALL DAY - WTI crude October futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Veritex Holdings (VBTX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VBTX]) will vote on the all-stock acquisition offer from Huntington Bancshares (HBAN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HBAN]). 

TUESDAY - SEPTEMBER 23

 	* ALL DAY - The three-day Qualcomm (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]) Snapdragon Summit is scheduled to begin in Maui, Hawaii. Announcements from the summit often set benchmarks for mobile, PC, and automotive processing, serving as a technology roadmap for Qualcomm’s (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]) partners and the broader ecosystem.
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Micron Technology (MU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU]), AutoZone (AZO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AZO]), and Worthington Enterprises (WOR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WOR]).
 	* ALL DAY - Global flash PMI reports will be the focus of economists.
 	* ALL DAY - SPS Commerce (SPSC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPSC]) will host its Investor Day event. The management team will provide an overview of strategic investments and growth opportunities.

WEDNESDAY - SEPTEMBER 24

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Cintas (CTAS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CTAS]), KB Home (KBH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KBH]), and Thor Industries (THO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/THO]).
 	* ALL DAY - Notable investor events include Guardant Health's (GH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GH]) Investor Day and Phillips Edison & Company's (PECO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PECO]) business update call.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Verona Pharma (VRNA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VRNA]) will vote on the $10B acquisition offer from Merck (MRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRK]). 
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with TaskUs (TASK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TASK]) will vote on the acquisition offer from a group led by the company's co-founders and Blackstone (BX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BX]). TaskUs (TASK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TASK]) traded 6% above the $16.50 deal price at the time of publication.
 	* 4:00 P.M. Nasdaq will release its latest update on short interest data.
 	* 4:10 P.M. San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly will speak on the economic outlook before the annual Spencer Fox Eccles Convocation.

THURSDAY - SEPTEMBER 25

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Costco Wholesale (NASDAQ:COST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COST]), Accenture (ACN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ACN]), and Jabil (JBL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JBL]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for CarMax (KMX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KMX]) after it reports.
 	* ALL DAY - It is the FDA action date for Crinetics Pharmaceuticals' Paltusotine, which has received Orphan Drug Designation for the acromegaly indication.
 	* ALL DAY - Pure Storage (PSTG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSTG]) will hold its Pure//Accelerate NYC event. CEO Charlie Giancarlo and founder John Colgrove will both give talks, and there will be a special meeting for financial analysts.
 	* ALL DAY - Notable investor events include Okta's (OKTA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKTA]) Investor Meeting, Klaviyo's (KVYO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KVYO]) Investor Day, and Malibu Boats (MBUU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MBUU]) Investor Day. Klaviyo's (KVYO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KVYO]) event is being held in conjunction with K:BOS, the company's flagship event for global consumer businesses and partners. Malibu Boats said its program will be comprised of presentations and discussions with the leadership team and will include an update on the company's strategy, long-term financial targets, and capital allocation priorities.
 	* 8:20 A.M. Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will participate in a moderated question-and-answer session before Crain's Power Lunch, "The Fed and the Economy: Trends for West Michigan."

FRIDAY - SEPTEMBER 26

 	* ALL DAY - The Texas Auto Show will begin as part of the State Fair of Texas in Dallas. A wide range of major automakers are expected to participate.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The core PCE price index report for August will be released. The Fed's favored inflation gauge was up 2.9% year-over-year in July and 2.8% in June. Economists forecast a 3.0% mark for August.

MORE ON THE MARKETS 

 	* The Current Round Of Rate Cuts Will Likely Spur The Global De-Dollarization Trend [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4824387-the-current-round-of-rate-cuts-will-likely-spur-the-global-de-dollarization-trend]
 	* Fed Cuts Its Target Interest Rate And Signals More To Come [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4824399-fed-cuts-target-interest-rate-signals-more-to-come]
 	* Expect Interest Rates To Plunge As We Barrel Toward A Recession [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4824364-expect-interest-rates-to-plunge-as-we-barrel-toward-a-recession]
 	* Trump administration said to mull using Japan's $550B fund to spur US manufacturing [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4496427-trump-administration-plan-us-manufacturing-boost]
 	* SEC won't block IPOs of firms that ban shareholder class action lawsuits [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4495918-sec-policy-statement-mandatory-arbitration-clauses]
